name: 1. Pin ℂ to a specific object. There's no such thing as the bare abstract category — a category is a structure, not a unique thing, so ℂ/~ has no referent until ℂ is named. 2. Make  compute the tuple from ℂ's data, not from a tuple you wrote by hand. 3. Show the 17.28M addresses are realized, not merely available. 3³×4⁵×5⁴ is the size of the grid — the codomain. For Crystal = ℂ/~ you need the projection onto: every address inhabited by a real object of ℂ. 4. Resolve the decode∘encode dilemma — the keystone. Fix only this and the centerpiece becomes a real theorem. Turn the folding/unfolding table into one proof. Force the value-counts, or name the ouroboros decoration.

phase_0.domain_name: 1. Pin ℂ to a specific object. There's no such thing as the bare abstract category — a category is a structure, not a unique thing, so ℂ/~ has no referent until ℂ is named. 2. Make  compute the tuple from ℂ's data, not from a tuple you wrote by hand. 3. Show the 17.28M addresses are realized, not merely available. 3³×4⁵×5⁴ is the size of the grid — the codomain. For Crystal = ℂ/~ you need the projection onto: every address inhabited by a real object of ℂ. 4. Resolve the decode∘encode dilemma — the keystone. Fix only this and the centerpiece becomes a real theorem. Turn the folding/unfolding table into one proof. Force the value-counts, or name the ouroboros decoration.
phase_0.boundary_condition: the closure of all proofs under the projection onto the 12-primitive basis

VINIT.chosen_element: the empty context, before any category is named
VINIT.justification: void/uninitialized state before any object or morphism exists

TANCH.chosen_element: the 12-primitive basis itself as a closed system
TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains the entire quotient and seals the crystal

AFWD.chosen_element: crystal_encode: projection of a category object onto its 12-tuple
AFWD.justification: forward morphism from the category to the crystal address space

AREV.chosen_element: crystal_decode: reconstruction of an equivalence class from its 12-tuple
AREV.justification: reverse morphism from the crystal back to the category fiber

CLINK.chosen_element: sequential composition of categorical operations (limits, colimits, adjunctions, etc.)
CLINK.justification: chaining of morphisms within the derivation

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the Yoneda embedding of an object — its self-recognition as a representable functor
IMSCRIB.justification: identity morphism that survives the quotient as a self-referential 12-tuple

FSPLIT.chosen_element: the evaluation of whether a given limit/colimit exists and is universal
FSPLIT.justification: splits into two arms: the true arm where the limit exists, the false arm where it diverges

FFUSE.chosen_element: the reconstitution of the original categorical operation from the split results
FFUSE.justification: reunites the true and false arms to recover the original operation exactly

EVALT.chosen_element: the state where the limit exists and the colimit is universal

EVALF.chosen_element: the state where the limit diverges or the colimit fails to be universal

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the dagger structure — an operation that is simultaneously a forward morphism and its reverse
ENGAGR.justification: paradice: both arms active simultaneously, held without resolution

IFIX.chosen_element: the mixed-radix encoding of a 12-tuple as a 27×1024×625 address
IFIX.justification: permanent, append-only record of the equivalence class

phase_2.split_input: a categorical operation (e.g., a limit diagram)
split_outputs: the true arm: limit exists and is universal
the false arm: limit diverges or fails
phase_2.fuse_element: the reconstitution of the original operation
phase_2.fuse_result: the original categorical operation, exactly as it was before the split

phase_3.void_description: the bare abstract category ℂ before any projection — no crystal address assigned
phase_3.true_description: a successful construction: limit exists, colimit universal, 12-tuple computed
phase_3.false_description: a failed construction: limit diverges, colimit non-universal, no valid 12-tuple
phase_3.both_description: the dagger structure: an operation that is both forward and reverse, held as a paradice

steps.domain_action: the empty context — no category named yet
crystal_encode — project the first object of ℂ onto its 12-tuple
evaluate whether the limit of this object exists
true arm — limit exists, colimit universal
chain the logical operations L1–L6 on the true arm
false arm — limit diverges, colimit fails
chain the failed operations on the false arm
dagger structure — both arms active simultaneously
reconstitute the original object from the split results
Yoneda embedding — the object recognizes itself
crystal_decode — recover the equivalence class from the 12-tuple
encode the 12-tuple as a permanent mixed-radix address
seal the crystal — the 12-primitive basis is closed

phase_5.compiler_frontend: crystal_encode: the projection map from ℂ to the 12-tuple address space
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: the 12-primitive basis: components communicate by sharing 12-tuple addresses
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the 17,280,000 address space, each address stored as a 27×1024×625 mixed-radix number
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the 4-stage derivation: L1–L6 → I1–I4 → A1–A5 in sequence
phase_5.alfs_store: the Lean-verified bijection crystal_decode ∘ crystal_encode = id, serving as the canonical reference store

phase_6.cycle_cost: the intra-fiber richness of ℂ that is forgotten by the projection
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: the full categorical structure of ℂ with all 15 operations
phase_6.post_cycle_state: the 12-tuple equivalence class, with intra-fiber details recoverable on demand
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the quotient map is structurally lossless; the bijection guarantees exact recovery

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → CLINK → EVALF → CLINK → ENGAGR → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → AREV → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: 1. Pin ℂ to a specific object. There's no such thing as the bare abstract category — a category is a structure, not a unique thing, so ℂ/~ has no referent until ℂ is named. 2. For Crystal = ℂ/~ you need the projection onto: every address inhabited by a real object of ℂ. 4. Resolve the decode∘encode dilemma — the keystone. -- Fingerprint: sig=(7,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=13
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(2, 8)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [4] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [5] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [6] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [7] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑗  | identity — self-imscription
--   [10] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑠 → 𐑭  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑗 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [12] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def 1_pin_to_a_specific_object_theres_no_such_thing_as_the_bare_abstract_category_a_category_is_a_structure_not_a_unique_thing_so_has_no_referent_until_is_named_2_make_compute_the_tuple_from_s_data_not_from_a_tuple_you_wrote_by_hand_3_show_the_17_28m_addresses_are_realized_not_merely_available_3_4_5_is_the_size_of_the_grid_the_codomain_for_crystal_you_need_the_projection_onto_every_address_inhabited_by_a_real_object_of_4_resolve_the_decode_encode_dilemma_the_keystone_fix_only_this_and_the_centerpiece_becomes_a_real_theorem_turn_the_folding_unfolding_table_into_one_proof_force_the_value_counts_or_name_the_ouroboros_decoration_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (the empty context — no category named yet)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑚)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (crystal_encode — project the first object of ℂ onto its 12-tuple)
  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := 𐑚) (evaluate whether the limit of this object exists) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [3] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (true arm — limit exists, colimit universal)
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (chain the logical operations L1–L6 on the true arm)
      -- F-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (false arm — limit diverges, colimit fails)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (chain the failed operations on the false arm)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (dagger structure — both arms active simultaneously))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (reconstitute the original object from the split results)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [9] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Yoneda embedding — the object recognizes itself)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [10] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (crystal_decode — recover the equivalence class from the 12-tuple)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑗 𐑡)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (encode the 12-tuple as a permanent mixed-radix address)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [12] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (seal the crystal — the 12-primitive basis is closed)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def 1_pin_to_a_specific_object_theres_no_such_thing_as_the_bare_abstract_category_a_category_is_a_structure_not_a_unique_thing_so_has_no_referent_until_is_named_2_make_compute_the_tuple_from_s_data_not_from_a_tuple_you_wrote_by_hand_3_show_the_17_28m_addresses_are_realized_not_merely_available_3_4_5_is_the_size_of_the_grid_the_codomain_for_crystal_you_need_the_projection_onto_every_address_inhabited_by_a_real_object_of_4_resolve_the_decode_encode_dilemma_the_keystone_fix_only_this_and_the_centerpiece_becomes_a_real_theorem_turn_the_folding_unfolding_table_into_one_proof_force_the_value_counts_or_name_the_ouroboros_decoration_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=

-- false arm
